basicDetails.schemeName: Chief Minister’s Award for Excellence in Public Administration

nodalDepartmentName.label: Personnel and Administrative Reforms Department, Puducherry

schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme aims to recognize and reward innovative and extraordinary work done by a team of officials to encourage good governance, high standards of services, and replication of best practices. It recognizes outstanding and exemplary performance by a team of officials with a scroll and cash award.
schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The scheme "Chief Minister’s Award for Excellence in Public Administration" was launched by the Department of Personnel and Administrative Reforms (DPAR), Union Territory of Puducherry. The scheme aims to recognize outstanding and exemplary performance by a team of officials under the Union Territory of Puducherry for achievements of initiatives and projects whose qualitative and quantitative outcomes/results are of very high order and which have benefitted a large number of citizens. The applications for this scheme are accepted by the Secretary to the Government (Administrative Reforms Wing), Chief Secretariat, Puducherry-605001.
schemeContent.benefits_md: ##### ***Chief Minister's Award for Excellence in Public Administration*** - The award carries a Commendation Scroll and a Cash Award of ₹10,00,000/- each. - Two such awards are given: one each for Departments’ and Organizations/Institutions’ categories. ##### ***Chief Minister's Appreciation for Initiative in Public Administration*** - The award carries a Commendation Scroll and a Cash Award of ₹5,00,000/- each. - Two such awards are given: one each for Departments’ and Organizations/Institutions’ categories. ##### ***Conditions*** - The amount of the cash award can be varied by the Government from time to time subject to the availability of funds. - The Government reserves the right not to present an Award in any year.
schemeContent.exclusions_md: - Self-nomination will summarily be rejected at an early stage itself. - Canvassing in any form at any point of time will not be entertained and such departments or organizations will be debarred as may be decided by the Chief Secretary.

children.text: The scheme "Chief Minister’s Award for Excellence in Public Administration" was launched by the Department of Personnel and Administrative Reforms (DPAR), Union Territory of Puducherry. The scheme aims to recognize outstanding and exemplary performance by a team of officials under the Union Territory of Puducherry for achievements of initiatives and projects whose qualitative and quantitative outcomes/results are of very high order and which have benefitted a large number of citizens. The applications for this scheme are accepted by the Secretary to the Government (Administrative Reforms Wing), Chief Secretariat, Puducherry-605001.
Chief Minister's Award for Excellence in Public Administration
The award carries a Commendation Scroll and a Cash Award of ₹10,00,000/- each.
Two such awards are given: one each for Departments’ and Organizations/Institutions’ categories.
Chief Minister's Appreciation for Initiative in Public Administration
The award carries a Commendation Scroll and a Cash Award of ₹5,00,000/- each.
Two such awards are given: one each for Departments’ and Organizations/Institutions’ categories.
Conditions
The amount of the cash award can be varied by the Government from time to time subject to the availability of funds.
The Government reserves the right not to present an Award in any year.
Self-nomination will summarily be rejected at an early stage itself.
Canvassing in any form at any point of time will not be entertained and such departments or organizations will be debarred as may be decided by the Chief Secretary.
Step 1:
The application should be submitted in the
prescribed format
, ensuring it clearly mentions the approving authority with relevant details.
Step 2:

The applicant must prepare a narrative of the project/initiative (not more than 2,500 words) clearly bringing out the background, priorities, strategies adopted, innovative method used, period of implementation, outcome, exceptional achievements, sustainability, and replicability. The application must include supporting documents like field photographs (maximum 10) and flow charts detailing uniqueness and success story of the initiatives.
Step 3:

The Administrative Secretary of the Department must constitute a Departmental Committee to shortlist and verify facts before forwarding nominations. The application should be submitted along with the recommendation of the Departmental Committee and a document of proof for approval from the Administrative Secretary. Nominating authorities are requested to forward nominations if the initiatives/accomplishments are truly innovative, extraordinary, and outstanding in nature.
Step 4:
The nomination must be made ONLY in the format prescribed (in soft and hard copy) and mailed directly by Registered Post/Speed Post and email directly to the
Secretary to the Government (Administrative Reforms Wing), Chief Secretariat, Puducherry-605001
. The corresponding Email Address is
usar@py.gov.in
Post-Application Processes
Step 1:
Screening and Shortlisting of Nominations
The Screening Committee shall screen the applications in the first stage, considering, among other things, the efforts, impact/outcomes, constraints, sustainability, and scope for replication.
The Committee may conduct spot studies wherever required and will endeavor to shortlist sufficient nominations for consideration of the Empowered Committee with specific recommendations.
Step 2:
Consideration by the Empowered Committee
The recommendations made by the Screening Committee shall be considered by an Empowered Committee.

The Empowered Committee may ask the nominees to make a presentation on their respective projects/works if required, and it would take into account the study reports and make its recommendations for the approval of the Chief Minister.
Note:
The Chief Secretary may permit a one-stage selection process through the Empowered Committee if the number of applications is less.
Application Deadlines
Receipt of nomination: 15th June.
Recommendation by Screening Committee: 7th July.
Recommendation by Empowered Committee: 31st July.
Announcement and Distribution of Awards: 25th August.
Note:
In case of insufficient nominations, the receipt of nominations may be extended.

applicationProcess.process_md:
**Step 1:** The application should be submitted in the [prescribed format](https://dpar.py.gov.in/CIRCULARS/GO.1_2025_ARW.pdf), ensuring it clearly mentions the approving authority with relevant details. **Step 2:** The applicant must prepare a narrative of the project/initiative (not more than 2,500 words) clearly bringing out the background, priorities, strategies adopted, innovative method used, period of implementation, outcome, exceptional achievements, sustainability, and replicability. The application must include supporting documents like field photographs (maximum 10) and flow charts detailing uniqueness and success story of the initiatives. **Step 3:** The Administrative Secretary of the Department must constitute a Departmental Committee to shortlist and verify facts before forwarding nominations. The application should be submitted along with the recommendation of the Departmental Committee and a document of proof for approval from the Administrative Secretary. Nominating authorities are requested to forward nominations if the initiatives/accomplishments are truly innovative, extraordinary, and outstanding in nature. **Step 4:** The nomination must be made ONLY in the format prescribed (in soft and hard copy) and mailed directly by Registered Post/Speed Post and email directly to the ***Secretary to the Government (Administrative Reforms Wing), Chief Secretariat, Puducherry-605001***. The corresponding Email Address is ***usar@py.gov.in***.
##### ***Post-Application Processes*** **Step 1:** **Screening and Shortlisting of Nominations** - The Screening Committee shall screen the applications in the first stage, considering, among other things, the efforts, impact/outcomes, constraints, sustainability, and scope for replication. - The Committee may conduct spot studies wherever required and will endeavor to shortlist sufficient nominations for consideration of the Empowered Committee with specific recommendations. **Step 2:** **Consideration by the Empowered Committee** - The recommendations made by the Screening Committee shall be considered by an Empowered Committee. - The Empowered Committee may ask the nominees to make a presentation on their respective projects/works if required, and it would take into account the study reports and make its recommendations for the approval of the Chief Minister. **Note:** The Chief Secretary may permit a one-stage selection process through the Empowered Committee if the number of applications is less. ##### ***Application Deadlines*** - Receipt of nomination: 15th June. - Recommendation by Screening Committee: 7th July. - Recommendation by Empowered Committee: 31st July. - Announcement and Distribution of Awards: 25th August. **Note:** In case of insufficient nominations, the receipt of nominations may be extended.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: - The applicant must represent one of the following: a) A Local Body under the UT of Puducherry. b) An Autonomous Body under the UT of Puducherry. c) A Corporation under the UT of Puducherry. d) An Agency under the UT of Puducherry. e) A Public Sector Undertaking under the UT of Puducherry. f) A Government Department under the UT of Puducherry. - The applicant must be nominated only through the Administrative Secretary. - The applicant must submit an application that contains the recommendation of the Departmental Committee. - The applicant must propose achievements of initiatives and projects whose qualitative and quantitative outcomes/results are of very high order and which have benefitted a large number of citizens. - The application should be made for rewardable work done during the previous one calendar year as on the date of nomination.

children.text: The applicant must represent one of the following:
a)      A Local Body under the UT of Puducherry.
b)      An Autonomous Body under the UT of Puducherry.
c)      A Corporation under the UT of Puducherry.
d)      An Agency under the UT of Puducherry.
e)      A Public Sector Undertaking under the UT of Puducherry.
f)      A Government Department under the UT of Puducherry.
The applicant must be nominated only through the Administrative Secretary.
The applicant must submit an application that contains the recommendation of the Departmental Committee.
The applicant must propose achievements of initiatives and projects whose qualitative and quantitative outcomes/results are of very high order and which have benefitted a large number of citizens.
The application should be made for rewardable work done during the previous one calendar year as on the date of nomination.
